title: Stripe wants to turn your AI costs into a profit center
summary:
Stripe has unveiled a new billing feature aimed at AI startups and other companies, designed to help them manage the costs associated with AI model usage more effectively. This innovative tool allows businesses to not only pass through the underlying costs of AI tokens to their customers but also to apply a markup percentage on these costs. For example, a startup can set an automatic 30% markup on the token costs, ensuring a consistent profit margin while simplifying the billing process. This feature is particularly beneficial for companies that rely on large language models (LLMs) and need to maintain profitability amidst fluctuating usage rates.

The billing feature is designed to track API prices of various AI models, allowing startups to select which models they wish to utilize. It automatically records customer token usage and applies the predetermined profit-margin markup, streamlining the financial management of AI services. This capability addresses a significant challenge faced by AI startups: the risk of incurring substantial costs when customers utilize their services extensively. Without a proper billing strategy, startups could find themselves operating at a loss, particularly when customer demand surges.

Moreover, the introduction of this feature comes at a time when many AI startups are exploring diverse pricing strategies, including tiered subscriptions and usage caps. The flexibility offered by Stripe's new tool could lead to more sustainable business models in the AI sector, enabling startups to scale while managing their operational costs effectively. As the AI landscape continues to evolve, solutions like Stripe's billing feature will be crucial for startups aiming to thrive in a competitive market.
social_text: Stripe's new billing feature allows AI startups to charge a markup on token usage, ensuring a consistent profit margin. Startups can set a 30% markup, automating billing and tracking API prices for models like OpenAI and Google Gemini.

title: The Side Gig Threshold Where Taxes Start To Matter More
summary:
The rise of side gigs has become a popular way for individuals to supplement their income, but it also brings with it a complex web of tax implications that many are unaware of. As side hustles grow, workers may inadvertently cross financial thresholds that trigger tax liabilities. Experts emphasize the importance of understanding when side income becomes taxable, noting that net earnings of $400 or more typically require self-employment tax reporting. Additionally, income exceeding $600 is often reported on a 1099 form by various platforms, which can lead to unexpected tax bills for those who do not keep accurate records of their earnings.

Self-employment tax is a significant consideration for side hustlers, as it encompasses both the employer and employee portions of Social Security and Medicare taxes. This is particularly relevant for individuals who transition their side gig into a primary source of income, as they may face a higher tax burden than those earning only wages. Tax professionals advise that side hustlers should be proactive in understanding their tax obligations to avoid surprises at tax time.

Furthermore, the IRS operates on a 'pay-as-you-go' system, meaning that individuals who expect to owe $1,000 or more in taxes must make estimated quarterly payments to avoid penalties. This requirement underscores the importance of financial planning for side gig workers, as failing to account for these obligations can lead to significant financial strain. By staying informed and organized, side hustlers can navigate the complexities of taxation and ensure compliance while maximizing their earnings.
social_text: Side gigs can lead to unexpected tax liabilities. Net earnings of $400 trigger self-employment tax reporting, while income over $600 is reported on a 1099. Be proactive to avoid penalties and manage your tax obligations effectively.

title: Significant Insider Activity at East West Bancorp (EWBC) in February
summary:
In February 2026, East West Bancorp, Inc. (NASDAQ:EWBC) experienced notable insider trading activity, particularly involving its CEO, Ng Dominic. On February 4, Dominic sold 50,000 shares of the company, amounting to $5,890,752. This transaction resulted in a 6% decrease in his ownership value, raising questions about the company's future direction and the executive's confidence in its performance. Additionally, on February 6, Director Molly Campbell sold 1,800 shares valued at $221,058, which represented a significant 24% reduction in her ownership stake. These insider sales could indicate a strategic repositioning or personal financial decisions by the executives, which investors should monitor closely.

Earlier in January 2026, East West Bancorp reported impressive financial results for Q4 and the full year of 2025. The company achieved record-high revenue of $2.9 billion, reflecting a 12% increase year-over-year, while net income reached $1.3 billion, or $9.52 per diluted share, marking a 14% growth compared to the previous year. The strong performance was further underscored by a quarterly dividend increase of 33%, bringing it to $0.80 per share, which demonstrates the company's commitment to returning value to its shareholders.

Founded in 1973, East West Bancorp operates globally, providing a range of commercial and consumer banking services. While the company shows potential as an investment, some analysts suggest that certain AI stocks may offer greater upside with less risk. Investors are encouraged to evaluate these insider activities alongside the company's robust financial performance to make informed decisions regarding their investments in EWBC.

title: Goldman Sachs Cuts Pinterest (PINS) Target as RBC Downgrades Shares
summary:

(NYSE:PINS) has recently faced significant downgrades from major financial institutions, reflecting growing concerns over its revenue performance amid challenging macroeconomic conditions. On February 13, 2026, Goldman Sachs reduced its price target for Pinterest from $32 to $23 while maintaining a Buy rating. Analyst Eric Sheridan highlighted that the company's fourth-quarter results indicated revenue pressures, particularly from major retail advertisers. Despite these challenges, Pinterest has seen a healthy increase in its user base, especially among Gen-Z users, which could provide a silver lining for future growth.

In a parallel move, RBC Capital downgraded Pinterest's stock from Outperform to Sector Perform, slashing its price target from $38 to $17. The firm expressed disappointment in the company's fourth-quarter performance, attributing some of the struggles to tariffs impacting advertising budgets. RBC's analysis pointed out that Pinterest's earlier strategies aimed at enhancing product cycles and conversion rates have not yielded the expected financial benefits, raising questions about the company's current trajectory.

Founded in 2010, Pinterest operates as a visual discovery engine and social commerce platform based in California. While analysts acknowledge the potential for Pinterest as an investment, there is a growing sentiment that other sectors, particularly AI stocks, may offer better upside potential with less risk. This shift in focus reflects broader market trends and investor sentiment as they navigate the complexities of the current economic landscape.

title: Wendy’s Is Down Sharply—Is the Dividend a Bargain or Value Trap?
summary:
Wendy's recent financial performance has raised significant concerns among investors, particularly following its Q4 2025 earnings report. Despite beating earnings expectations, the company reported its worst same-store sales performance in two decades, leading to a steep decline in its stock price, which hit a 52-week low of $6.73. Over the past year, Wendy's shares have plummeted nearly 51%, and they have lost over 61% of their value in the last five years. This drastic downturn has prompted questions about the sustainability of its high dividend yield, which currently exceeds 7%. While this yield may attract income-focused investors, the company's weak growth outlook and declining free cash flow have raised red flags about the potential for a value trap.

In response to these challenges, Wendy's management is implementing several strategic initiatives, including store closures and a menu overhaul under the initiative dubbed 'Project Fresh.' The company plans to close between 5% and 6% of its locations in 2026, aiming to streamline operations and enhance profitability. Additionally, Wendy's is working to make its value menu more competitive, particularly with offerings like the Biggie Bag, to attract price-sensitive consumers amid a challenging economic environment.

Notably, hedge fund billionaire Nelson Peltz, a long-time shareholder, is exploring options to enhance shareholder value, including a potential takeover of the fast-food chain. However, the ongoing transformation efforts and the current market conditions make it uncertain whether these strategies will be sufficient to reverse the downward trend in Wendy's stock. Investors remain cautious as they weigh the risks and rewards associated with the company's future prospects.
social_text: Wendy's stock has dropped nearly 51% in the past year, hitting a 52-week low of $6.73. With a 7%+ dividend yield, concerns arise about a potential value trap amid declining free cash flow and poor sales performance.

title: UBS Upgrades Southwest Airlines (LUV) to Buy, Raises Target by $22
summary:
UBS has upgraded Southwest Airlines Co. (NYSE:LUV) from Neutral to Buy, increasing the price target from $51 to $73. This upgrade reflects UBS's confidence in Southwest's ability to surpass its initial targets for assigned seating and extra-legroom programs. The firm projects that these initiatives, along with the implementation of checked bag fees, could contribute an impressive $4.25 to $4.50 in incremental earnings per share by fiscal 2027. This positive outlook is indicative of the airline's strategic focus on enhancing customer experience and revenue generation.

In a significant move to improve onboard services, Southwest Airlines announced plans to integrate SpaceX's Starlink technology into its fleet, aiming to provide next-generation Wi-Fi across its network in 11 countries. Tony Roach, the airline's Executive Vice President and Chief Customer & Brand Officer, emphasized that Starlink will enable passengers to enjoy an at-home experience in the air, allowing them to stream shows, download music, and stay connected throughout their flights. The airline plans to equip over 300 aircraft with this technology by the end of 2026, marking a substantial investment in customer service.

Founded in 1967, Southwest Airlines has established itself as a major player in the U.S. airline industry, known for its low-cost, point-to-point travel model. While UBS's upgrade highlights the potential of LUV as a strong investment opportunity, the article also notes that certain AI stocks may present greater upside potential with less risk. This juxtaposition underscores the competitive landscape of investment opportunities available to investors in 2026, particularly in the evolving sectors of travel and technology.

title: Geopolitical drama reportedly stalls IPO of SoftBank-backed PayPay
summary:
PayPay, Japan's leading mobile payment application, has decided to postpone its initial public offering (IPO) in the United States due to ongoing market volatility and geopolitical tensions, particularly the recent conflict in the Middle East. Originally, the company planned to announce its IPO price range on March 2, aiming for a valuation of at least ¥1.5 trillion (approximately $10 billion). This delay reflects broader concerns in the tech sector, where several companies have either withdrawn or postponed their IPO plans amid a sell-off in software stocks, driven by fears of obsolescence due to advancements in artificial intelligence.

Founded in 2018 as a joint venture between SoftBank and Yahoo Japan, PayPay has rapidly established itself as a dominant player in Japan's mobile payment landscape. The company previously collaborated with India's Paytm, which sold its remaining stake to SoftBank for around $279 million in late 2024. The current market environment has been particularly challenging for tech IPOs, as investors remain cautious following a series of high-profile delays and withdrawals, including those of Motive Technologies and Clear Street.

Despite the setbacks, there remains optimism for future mega-IPOs in the tech sector, with public investors eyeing potential listings from companies like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic in 2026. The situation highlights the fragility of the current IPO landscape, where geopolitical events and market dynamics can significantly impact companies' plans to go public, especially in a climate where investor sentiment is increasingly influenced by global events.
social_text: PayPay postpones its US IPO due to market volatility and Middle East conflict, aiming for a valuation of ¥1.5 trillion ($10B). The tech IPO landscape remains shaky, with investors eyeing future mega-IPOs.

title: No one has a good plan for how AI companies should work with the government
summary:
Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, faced significant public scrutiny during a recent Q&A session on X after announcing the company's acceptance of a Pentagon contract that competitor Anthropic had declined. The contract raised concerns about OpenAI's potential involvement in mass surveillance and automated warfare, issues that Anthropic had explicitly rejected. Altman attempted to clarify OpenAI's position, emphasizing the importance of democratic processes and the role of elected officials in shaping policy. However, his responses revealed a disconnect between the tech industry and public sentiment regarding the ethical implications of AI in national security.

The situation highlights a critical juncture for OpenAI as it evolves from a consumer-focused startup into a key player in national security. Altman's comments indicated a reliance on traditional defense industry norms, where private companies defer to civilian leadership. Yet, the backlash from the public suggests that OpenAI may not be fully prepared to navigate the complexities and responsibilities that come with such a transition. This incident underscores the broader challenges faced by tech companies as they engage with government contracts and the ethical dilemmas that arise from their technologies.

As OpenAI moves forward, it will need to address the concerns of the public and stakeholders regarding its role in national security. The company's ability to manage its responsibilities and communicate transparently will be crucial in maintaining trust and credibility. The ongoing debate about the balance of power between democratically elected governments and private corporations in the tech space is likely to intensify, making it imperative for OpenAI and similar companies to develop clear strategies for ethical engagement with government entities.
social_text: Sam Altman faced backlash after OpenAI accepted a Pentagon contract, previously rejected by Anthropic, raising concerns about mass surveillance and automated warfare. Public sentiment questions the role of tech in national security.

title: 'She's In Real Trouble,' Dave Ramsey Says After 70-Year-Old Grandma Lands In Jail Over Four Credit Cards And $15K Withdrawal
summary:
In a troubling turn of events, a 70-year-old grandmother has found herself in legal trouble after being entrusted with her grandson's finances. Cody, a 25-year-old student from Wichita, Kansas, believed that giving his grandmother access to his checking account would help him stay focused on his studies and avoid financial pitfalls. However, this decision quickly spiraled out of control, leading to unpaid bills and serious allegations of identity theft involving his sister. The situation worsened following the recent death of Cody's grandfather, which may have contributed to his grandmother's erratic financial behavior.

Cody's grandmother withdrew a staggering $15,000 from his account shortly before the discovery of four credit cards opened in his sister's name. This alarming revelation prompted Cody's sister to file a police report, which ultimately led to their grandmother's arrest. Financial expert Dave Ramsey expressed concern over the situation, emphasizing that Cody, at 25, should have been managing his own finances rather than relying on someone else. The case highlights the potential risks of entrusting financial responsibilities to family members, especially in times of emotional distress.

As the legal ramifications unfold, the family faces not only financial turmoil but also the emotional strain of dealing with their grandmother's arrest. This incident serves as a cautionary tale about the importance of financial literacy and the potential consequences of poor financial decisions. It underscores the need for individuals to take charge of their own financial well-being, particularly during challenging life events, to prevent similar situations from arising in the future.
social_text: A 70-year-old grandmother was arrested after withdrawing $15K from her grandson's account and opening four credit cards in her daughter's name. Financial expert Dave Ramsey warns of the dangers of misplaced trust in family finances.

title: Einride raises $113M in oversubscribed PIPE ahead of SPAC merger
summary:
Einride, a Swedish company specializing in autonomous freight technology, has successfully raised $113 million through an oversubscribed private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing. This funding surpassed its initial target of $100 million, indicating a robust investor interest in the autonomous trucking sector. The capital raised is intended to support Einride's upcoming merger with Legato Merger Corp. III, which will bring the total committed investments to around $213 million, valuing the company at a pre-money equity valuation of $1.35 billion. The merger is projected to yield approximately $333 million in gross proceeds, which includes the PIPE financing and cash from Legato's trust, before accounting for potential redemptions and transaction costs.

The PIPE financing attracted both new and existing investors, including notable firms such as EQT Ventures and a prominent unnamed asset management company from the U.S. West Coast. Roozbeh Charli, CEO of Einride, emphasized that this investment reflects strong confidence in the company's mission to revolutionize global freight through autonomous and electric technologies. With this influx of capital, Einride aims to enhance its commercial deployments of electric and autonomous freight solutions, while also investing in its automated driving system and intelligent freight platform.

The proceeds from the PIPE financing will facilitate Einride's ambitious technology roadmap and global expansion plans, which include deploying autonomous solutions across North America, Europe, and the Middle East. The company is also focused on advancing its intelligent freight platform, which leverages AI for planning and optimization, and operates one of the largest electric heavy-duty fleets along with necessary charging infrastructure. This strategic move positions Einride at the forefront of the intersection of autonomy, electrification, and logistics, paving the way for significant advancements in the freight industry.
social_text: Einride raised $113M in an oversubscribed PIPE, exceeding its $100M target, ahead of its merger with Legato Merger Corp. III, valuing the company at $1.35B and supporting global expansion in autonomous freight solutions.

title: Nvidia Stock May Be Oversold - What is the Best NVDA Play?
summary:

This drop has raised questions about whether the stock is oversold, especially given Nvidia's strong financial performance. These strategies aim to capitalize on the potential rebound in Nvidia's stock price, particularly if the strong FCF margins continue as projected.

In conclusion, while Nvidia's stock may remain undervalued in the short term, the long-term outlook appears promising based on solid financial fundamentals. title: Crypto Loses Its Grip on Retail Crowd Now Defecting to Equities
summary:
The retail investor landscape is undergoing a significant transformation as interest in cryptocurrencies wanes, with many shifting their focus to equities. A recent report from market-maker Wintermute, utilizing data from JPMorgan Chase & Co., highlights this trend, which has been particularly pronounced since the October 2024 crypto crash. Retail investors, who have historically been the backbone of the crypto market, are now diverting their speculative investments into stocks, marking a departure from the previous trend where both asset classes moved in tandem based on risk appetite. This shift raises questions about the sustainability of the crypto market, which has relied heavily on retail enthusiasm for its growth.

The October crash, which resulted in over $19 billion in liquidations and affected more than 1.6 million traders, has acted as a catalyst for this pivot. As Bitcoin's value plummeted from approximately $126,000 to around $66,000, retail investors have increasingly turned to equities, seeking more stable investment opportunities backed by corporate earnings and dividends. This transition indicates a fundamental change in the market structure of cryptocurrencies, which have traditionally thrived on retail investor sentiment rather than institutional support.

Evgeny Gaevoy, CEO of Wintermute, notes that crypto is now just one of many risky asset classes available to retail investors, diminishing its previous status as a primary focus for speculative trading. The ongoing shift towards equities suggests that without a new catalyst to attract retail investors back into the crypto space, the digital asset market may struggle to recover and regain its previous momentum. This evolving landscape poses challenges for the future of cryptocurrencies as they compete for attention in a crowded investment arena.
social_text: Retail investors are pivoting from crypto to equities, driven by the October crash that wiped out $19B. Bitcoin fell from $126K to $66K, as stocks gain traction, reshaping market dynamics.

title: As Coinbase Rolls out 24/5 Stock Trading, Should You Snap up COIN?
summary:
Coinbase, a leading cryptocurrency exchange, has recently launched 24/5 stock trading, allowing users to trade U.S.-listed equities and ETFs around the clock on weekdays. This strategic move aims to enhance user engagement and attract a broader audience, especially retail investors who are increasingly seeking diverse investment options. The introduction of commission-free trading and features like fractional shares and instant funding positions Coinbase as a formidable competitor in the financial services sector, particularly against established players in the stock trading space.

Despite the recent downturn in Coinbase's stock price, which has seen a decline of nearly 59% from its July peak of $444.64, the company continues to innovate and expand its offerings. With a current market cap of $47.8 billion, Coinbase is not only focusing on cryptocurrency trading but is also venturing into traditional finance by integrating stock trading into its platform. CEO Brian Armstrong's vision of creating an “Everything Exchange” reflects the company's ambition to bridge the gap between traditional finance and the digital asset economy, potentially reshaping how users interact with financial markets.

As Coinbase rolls out these new features, investors are left to ponder whether this expansion will serve as a catalyst for recovery or if it is merely a gamble in an increasingly competitive landscape. The company's ability to adapt and innovate will be crucial in determining its future success, especially as it faces challenges from both crypto market volatility and competition from other trading platforms. Investors are now weighing the potential benefits of these new offerings against the backdrop of a challenging market environment.
social_text: Coinbase has launched 24/5 stock trading, offering over 8,000 U.S. equities and ETFs with $1 fractional shares. CEO Brian Armstrong aims to bridge traditional finance and digital assets, enhancing user engagement amid a 59% stock decline since July peak.

title: A new app alerts you if someone nearby is wearing smart glasses
summary:
The emergence of smart glasses equipped with recording capabilities has raised significant privacy concerns, as these devices often resemble ordinary eyewear, making it difficult for individuals to know when they are being recorded. In response to these concerns, a new Android app called Nearby Glasses has been developed to alert users when someone nearby is wearing smart glasses or other recording devices. This app continuously scans for Bluetooth signals emitted by wearable technology, particularly those manufactured by companies like Meta and Snap, which have faced scrutiny for their potential misuse in surveillance scenarios.

Yves Jeanrenaud, the creator of Nearby Glasses, was motivated to develop the app after observing the negative implications of wearable surveillance devices, particularly in instances where they have been used to infringe on the privacy of individuals without their consent. His inspiration was partly drawn from investigative reports highlighting the misuse of Meta's Ray-Bans smart glasses in sensitive situations, such as immigration enforcement and the harassment of marginalized groups. Jeanrenaud has expressed strong opposition to the intrusive nature of smart glasses, labeling them as a significant threat to personal privacy.

The functionality of Nearby Glasses relies on detecting unique Bluetooth identifiers associated with specific manufacturers. When the app identifies a signal from a nearby device, it promptly alerts the user, empowering them to be aware of their surroundings and the potential for being recorded. This innovative tool aims to foster a greater sense of privacy and awareness in an era where surveillance technology is becoming increasingly prevalent, reflecting a growing demand for transparency and consent in the use of recording devices.
social_text: The Nearby Glasses app alerts users when nearby smart glasses, like Meta's Ray-Bans, are detected via Bluetooth signals, addressing privacy concerns over unconsented recording. Developed by Yves Jeanrenaud, it empowers users in a surveillance-heavy environment.

title: Instagram tracked growing usage while targeting teens, lawyers argue
summary:
In a significant court case, Instagram's tracking of user engagement metrics is under scrutiny as it faces allegations of contributing to mental health issues among teens. The trial, K.G.M. v. Platforms et al., is currently taking place in Los Angeles County Superior Court, marking Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's first appearance before a jury. The case centers around a 19-year-old plaintiff, referred to as K.G.M., who claims that her use of social media from a young age led to addiction and severe mental health challenges, including depression and suicidal thoughts. The outcome of this trial could set a precedent for how social media companies are held accountable for the impact of their platforms on youth mental health.

The lawsuit raises critical questions about the responsibility of social media companies in monitoring and managing the effects of their platforms on young users. Lawyers representing K.G.M. argue that Meta intentionally aimed to increase user engagement, despite being aware of the presence of minors on Instagram. Internal documents revealed during the trial indicate that Instagram's daily usage grew significantly, from 40 minutes in 2023 to 46 minutes in 2026, suggesting a deliberate strategy to enhance user retention. In contrast, Meta maintains that the plaintiff's mental health struggles stem from various factors unrelated to social media use.

As the trial unfolds, the jury will consider whether Instagram's design and engagement strategies were substantial contributors to K.G.M.'s mental health issues. The case is particularly notable as Snap and TikTok settled prior to the trial, leaving Meta and YouTube to face the legal challenges head-on. The outcome could have far-reaching implications for the tech industry, particularly regarding how social media platforms are regulated and the measures they must take to protect young users from potential harm.
social_text: Instagram's daily usage rose from 40 minutes in 2023 to 46 minutes in 2026, raising concerns in K.G.M. v. Platforms et al. about its impact on youth mental health, as Meta faces allegations of fostering addiction among minors.

title: Forget BigBear.ai: This Cloud Platform Is Quietly Becoming Mission‑Critical for Fortune 500 AI Workloads
summary:

While BigBear.ai has carved out a niche in providing AI applications for government and defense sectors, IBM's recent acquisition of Confluent positions it as a key player in the cloud platform space for Fortune 500 companies. Confluent's capabilities in managing and processing vast amounts of data make it essential for businesses looking to leverage AI effectively. This acquisition highlights the increasing importance of robust data access and management solutions in the AI landscape.

BigBear.ai specializes in critical AI applications such as TrueFace, a facial recognition platform that operates with a remarkable 99.1% success rate against a database of 12 million identities. This technology is vital for security operations at airports and other sensitive locations. Additionally, their ConductorOS platform enhances security for edge computing, enabling military personnel to maintain secure communications in remote areas. Despite its significant contributions, BigBear.ai faces competition from larger entities like IBM, which are expanding their capabilities through strategic acquisitions.

As AI continues to evolve, the demand for reliable data access and processing solutions will only grow. IBM's acquisition of Confluent is a strategic move that underscores the necessity for Fortune 500 companies to adopt comprehensive cloud platforms that can support their AI workloads. This shift may redefine the competitive landscape, as businesses prioritize platforms that can seamlessly integrate data management with AI functionalities, ensuring they remain at the forefront of technological advancements.

title: ‘That’s not the function of money’: A viral TikTok explains how to really get rich, using a millionaire mindset
summary:
In a recent viral TikTok, billionaire Mark Cuban shared insights on wealth-building that challenge traditional financial wisdom. During an interview with Spanx founder Sara Blakely, Cuban recounted his own frugal lifestyle, emphasizing the importance of prioritizing savings and investments over luxury purchases. He described a time when he lived with five roommates, drove a dilapidated car, and subsisted on mac and cheese, all in pursuit of financial independence by the age of 35. Cuban's approach highlights a mindset focused on valuing time and experiences over material possessions, suggesting that true wealth comes from making strategic financial decisions rather than indulging in consumerism.

Cuban's philosophy aligns with the views of financial educator Conroy, who argues that the average middle-class mindset differs significantly from that of millionaires. Conroy posits that money should be viewed as a tool for expansion rather than a means to acquire luxury items. He encourages individuals to shift their perspective on money, advocating for its use in generating further income and opportunities. This approach is a departure from the conventional strategy of accumulating wealth through savings and material upgrades.

The discussion serves as a reminder that financial success is not solely about earning more but also about how one manages and invests their resources. By adopting a millionaire mindset, individuals can unlock new opportunities for wealth creation, moving beyond the limitations of traditional financial habits. Cuban's insights resonate with many aspiring entrepreneurs and investors, inspiring them to rethink their relationship with money and focus on long-term growth strategies.
social_text: Mark Cuban emphasizes a millionaire mindset, advocating for frugality and investment over luxury. He lived with five roommates and drove a beat-up car to prioritize savings, aiming for financial independence by 35. Shift your perspective on money to focus on expansion.

title: Why Is The Crypto Market Up Today?
summary:
The recent surge in the cryptocurrency market can be attributed to geopolitical events, specifically the announcement by US President Donald Trump regarding the elimination of Iran's Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei. This news has positively impacted market sentiment, leading to a $23 billion increase in the total crypto market cap, which now stands at $2.27 trillion. Bitcoin has also seen a significant rebound, reclaiming the $65,000 mark, indicating renewed investor confidence. Altcoins, particularly LayerZero (ZRO), have followed suit with impressive gains, highlighting a broader bullish trend across the market.

In addition to the geopolitical factors, institutional interest in the crypto space is growing. Morgan Stanley has applied for a national trust bank charter to custody digital assets, which would allow them to hold and manage cryptocurrencies for clients. This move signifies a deeper commitment to expanding their services in the crypto sector and could attract more institutional investors, further bolstering market stability and growth.

However, not all news is positive. In Minnesota, lawmakers have introduced a bill aimed at banning crypto kiosks due to a rise in scams targeting vulnerable populations, particularly older residents. This regulatory scrutiny could pose challenges for the crypto market, as it may lead to increased oversight and potential restrictions. Overall, while the market is currently experiencing a bullish phase, its future trajectory will depend on both macroeconomic conditions and regulatory developments.
social_text: The crypto market cap surged by $23B to $2.27T after Trump announced the elimination of Iran's Supreme Leader. Bitcoin hit $65K, while Morgan Stanley seeks a charter for digital asset custody, signaling institutional growth.

title: Coupang (CPNG) Records $8.8B Q4 Revenue, $26M Loss Amid Data Breach Backlash
summary:
Coupang Inc. (NYSE:CPNG) reported its fourth-quarter earnings for 2025, revealing a revenue of $8.8 billion, which fell short of the $8.9 billion consensus estimate. The company also faced a $26 million loss during this quarter, a significant decline from the profit it reported in the same period last year. This downturn can be attributed to a decrease in active customers, which dropped to 24.6 million from 24.7 million in the previous quarter. The decline in customer numbers is likely linked to the backlash following a data breach that affected approximately 34 million customers in November, raising concerns about data security and customer trust in the brand. Additionally, Coupang reported a diluted loss per share of $0.01, which did not meet the anticipated earnings per share of $0.04, further highlighting the challenges the company is currently facing.

In response to these disappointing results, BofA Securities adjusted its price target for Coupang, lowering it from $32 to $28 while maintaining a Buy rating. The downgrade reflects concerns over the company's slowing growth trajectory, which has been exacerbated by the recent data breach incident. Despite these challenges, analysts still recognize Coupang's potential as a long-term investment, particularly given its diverse offerings in retail, restaurant delivery, video streaming, and fintech services. However, the market's reaction to the earnings report and the data breach may influence investor sentiment in the short term.

As Coupang navigates these turbulent waters, it remains crucial for the company to address the data security issues and restore customer confidence. The ongoing scrutiny from investors and analysts will likely shape its strategic decisions moving forward. While some experts suggest that other AI stocks may present better opportunities, Coupang's position in the market and its ability to adapt to challenges will be key factors in determining its future performance and growth potential.
social_text: Coupang Inc. reported $8.8B in Q4 revenue, missing estimates, and a $26M loss due to a data breach affecting 34M customers. BofA cut its price target to $28, citing growth slowdown. Active customers fell to 24.6M.

summary:

title: Tamarack Valley Energy Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
summary:
Tamarack Valley Energy reported strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025, showcasing a robust operational performance despite lower WTI prices. The company achieved an adjusted fund flow of CAD 172 million in Q4, with capital expenditures totaling CAD 99 million and free fund flow reaching CAD 71 million. For the entire year, Tamarack generated approximately CAD 390 million in free fund flow, translating to CAD 0.78 per basic share, marking a 10% increase from the previous year. This growth is particularly notable given that WTI prices averaged 14% lower in 2025 compared to the prior year, highlighting the company's resilience and effective cost management strategies.

Production figures also reflected Tamarack's operational success, with Q4 production averaging 68,635 BOE/d, a 4% increase from Q4 2024, and a 6% year-over-year increase in full-year production averaging 68,176 BOE/d. This performance exceeded the company's revised guidance range of 67,000 to 69,000 BOE/d, demonstrating effective execution and strategic focus on core assets. The company successfully divested 4,000 BOE/d of non-core production in mid-October, allowing for a more streamlined operational focus on Clearwater and Charlie Lake plays.

Management emphasized the completion of a multi-year transformation into a dedicated conventional oil producer, which has led to significant operational efficiencies and cost reductions. Net operating expenses fell by 17% to CAD 7.43/BOE, with a target of CAD 7.00/BOE for 2026. Tamarack also achieved its net debt target of 1.0x net debt-to-EBITDA at US$50 WTI, positioning the company for continued growth and stability in the coming years. The executives expressed confidence in the company's strategic direction and operational capabilities, setting the stage for future success.
social_text: Tamarack Valley Energy reported Q4 adjusted fund flow of CAD 172M and full-year free fund flow of CAD 390M, achieving CAD 0.78/share. Production averaged 68,635 BOE/d, exceeding guidance despite lower WTI prices.

title: How America’s Got Talent host Howie Mandel turned 2 acres of dirt into his ‘best investment’ outside showbiz
summary:
Howie Mandel, best known as the host of America's Got Talent, has successfully ventured into commercial real estate, transforming a two-acre plot of land into a lucrative investment. Recognizing the potential for growth in Las Vegas during the 1980s, Mandel strategically purchased land that would serve the needs of a burgeoning community, anticipating the influx of residents from new housing developments. His foresight led him to build essential services such as a gas station, an AMPM store, and a car wash, catering to the needs of approximately 2,000 families in the area.

Mandel's journey into real estate began with investments in warehouses, but he quickly pivoted to focus on commercial properties that would yield higher returns. The rapid development in Las Vegas, coupled with favorable tax conditions in Nevada, allowed him to capitalize on the growing demand for commercial spaces. His investments have proven fruitful, as he noted that he couldn't build fast enough to meet the demand from new residents and businesses in the area.

Today, Mandel's portfolio includes various commercial properties, with gas stations emerging as a significant part of his business. He emphasizes the dual benefit of his investments, as they not only provide financial returns but also foster a creative environment for tech companies and other innovators. By creating spaces that encourage collaboration among creatives, Mandel has successfully blended his passion for entertainment with a savvy business acumen in real estate.

title: Woman quit her job to care for her sick mother and is now working 4-day weeks at age 72 to make ends meet
summary:
Susan Freeman, a 72-year-old woman, reflects on the financial repercussions of her decision to leave the workforce to care for her ailing mother. While she does not regret her choice, it has significantly impacted her financial independence and retirement prospects. Before becoming a full-time caregiver, Freeman had a diverse career in banking, insurance, and even owned a pizzeria. However, as her mother's health deteriorated following a stroke, Freeman sold her business and relied heavily on Social Security disability checks and her husband's income, leading to financial strain and a temporary separation from her husband.

Freeman's mother eventually moved to a nursing home in 2015 and passed away in 2019, but the years spent as a caregiver left Freeman with limited savings and the need to continue working at a family-owned uniform store. Despite the challenges, Freeman expresses pride in having cared for her mother, though she acknowledges the sacrifices made. She emphasizes the importance of considering one's own financial future when taking on caregiving responsibilities, especially for those nearing retirement age.

The article highlights the broader implications of caregiving on financial stability, particularly as the number of caregivers in the U.S. is expected to rise. According to an Edward Jones survey, two in five U.S. adults currently identify as caregivers, a figure projected to increase to 46% in the coming years. This trend underscores the need for individuals to weigh the financial pros and cons of caregiving and to develop strategies to protect their retirement plans while fulfilling familial obligations.
social_text: At 72, Susan Freeman works four days a week at a uniform store after leaving her job to care for her mother, impacting her retirement savings. Two in five U.S. adults are caregivers, projected to rise to 46%.

title: Advisers Weigh Gold’s Value After Volatility Tarnishes Record Run-up
summary:
The financial advisory community is currently divided on the value of gold as an investment option, particularly following its recent volatility. While some advisors view gold as a minor allocation that provides non-correlated portfolio ballast, others are deterred by its lack of income generation and dividends. Despite these differing opinions, gold's performance over the past few years has been remarkable, with a nearly 64% gain in 2025 and a climb of over 26% the previous year. Notably, gold has outperformed the S&P 500 Index in three of the last four years, showcasing its potential as a valuable asset in investment portfolios.

However, the recent surge in gold prices, which peaked at an all-time high of $5,589 an ounce on January 28, was followed by a significant decline of nearly 18% within three trading days. This sharp drop has prompted financial advisors to reassess their clients' allocations to gold. Despite this volatility, gold remains up nearly 20% since the beginning of the year, leading some advisors to view the pullback as a potential buying opportunity for long-term investors. The structural factors supporting gold's value, such as central bank accumulation and geopolitical risks, remain intact.

Advisors like Sam Diarbakerly from Generation Capital Advisors emphasize that the recent dip should not deter investors with a long-term perspective. He suggests that the current market conditions, including elevated central bank purchases and ongoing de-dollarization, could lead to a resurgence in gold prices. Industry forecasts indicate that gold may finish the year as much as 20% higher than its current price, reinforcing the notion that the asset could still play a significant role in diversified investment strategies.
social_text: Gold's value is under scrutiny after a peak of $5,589 an ounce followed by an 18% drop. Advisors see potential for a 20% year-end increase, driven by central bank buying and geopolitical risks.

title: Source Energy Services Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
summary:
Source Energy Services reported a strong performance in its fourth-quarter earnings call, highlighting a notable rebound in activity levels. The company achieved well site solutions revenue of CAD 28.3 million, an increase of CAD 1.6 million year-over-year, primarily driven by higher last-mile logistics volumes and longer trips to well sites. Despite challenges in commodity prices, the company managed to close 2025 with record annual volumes and revenue, demonstrating resilience in a fluctuating market. The fourth-quarter sand revenue reached CAD 135.3 million, marking a CAD 17.7 million increase compared to the previous year, attributed to the completion of delayed projects from the third quarter of 2025.

CEO Scott Melbourn and CFO Derren Newell discussed the key cost and margin drivers during the earnings call, noting that while sales volume increased to 907,000 tons—an 18% rise from the fourth quarter of 2024—margins faced pressure. The adjusted gross margin per ton fell to CAD 39.07 from CAD 44.88 the previous year, and adjusted EBITDA decreased to CAD 112.3 million. Factors contributing to this decline included shifts in product and terminal mix, increased startup costs, and higher maintenance expenses, alongside adverse weather conditions.

Looking ahead to 2026, Source Energy Services anticipates relatively flat volumes with steadier quarter-to-quarter activity. The company has guided net capital expenditures between CAD 30-40 million and ended 2025 with available liquidity of CAD 59.9 million after executing share buybacks and reducing a term loan by CAD 23.7 million. The management remains optimistic about navigating the market landscape while maintaining operational efficiency and financial stability.
social_text: Source Energy Services reported Q4 sand revenue of CAD 135.3M (+13.5% YoY) with sales volume of 907K tons (+18% YoY). Adjusted EBITDA fell to CAD 112.3M due to margin pressures and increased costs. Liquidity stands at CAD 59.9M.

title: Medicare 101: New book explains what most of us don't know about healthcare in retirement
summary:
In her new book 'Medicare 101', financial journalist Kimberly Lankford provides a comprehensive guide to navigating the complexities of Medicare, a crucial aspect of healthcare for retirees. The book breaks down the various parts of Medicare, including Part A for hospital care, Part B for medical services, Part C for Medicare Advantage, and Part D for prescription drug coverage. Lankford emphasizes the importance of understanding these options to make informed decisions that can significantly impact one's healthcare costs in retirement.

One of the key takeaways from Lankford's insights is the necessity for individuals to actively enroll in Medicare, especially if they are not already receiving Social Security benefits. The enrollment process can be daunting, with a seven-month window around the 65th birthday that includes three months before, the month of, and three months after. Missing this window can result in lifelong penalties for late enrollment in Parts B and D, which can lead to higher monthly premiums.

Lankford also highlights common pitfalls that many face when signing up for Medicare, particularly those still working. She advises consulting with HR departments to understand how employer health plans interact with Medicare coverage. For those employed by smaller companies, signing up for Parts A and B is often necessary, while those with larger employers may continue their existing health insurance as primary coverage. Overall, 'Medicare 101' serves as an essential resource for anyone approaching retirement age, ensuring they are well-prepared for their healthcare needs.
social_text: Kimberly Lankford's 'Medicare 101' demystifies Medicare enrollment, highlighting the 7-month sign-up window and costly pitfalls like late penalties. Essential for retirees navigating healthcare options.
